content: Statin adverse effects: myalgias (muscle pain without CK elevation) common (5-10% in practice, similar to placebo in blinded trials—nocebo effect significant); myopathy (muscle symptoms with CK >10x ULN) rare (0.1%); rhabdomyolysis very rare (<0.01%). Risk factors for myopathy: advanced age, female sex, low BMI, renal/hepatic impairment, hypothyroidism, vitamin D deficiency, drug interactions (CYP3A4 inhibitors with simvastatin/lovastatin/atorvastatin; gemfibrozil). Management: check baseline CK only if high risk; if symptoms develop, check CK, rule out other causes (hypothyroidism, vitamin D deficiency); if CK normal/mildly elevated, continue or rechallenge; if CK >10x ULN, stop statin. New-onset diabetes: slight increased risk (9% relative increase, ~0.1% absolute per year), primarily in predisposed patients; CV benefits far outweigh diabetes risk. Liver enzyme elevation: transient mild ALT/AST elevation common; check baseline LFTs, repeat only if symptoms; true hepatotoxicity extremely rare. Cognitive effects: no consistent evidence of cognitive decline; FDA removed warning in 2019.
